sourceMovie.title: Predestination
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Predestination for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Michael Spierig keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The science fiction and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Minority Report
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Predestination clicked for you, Minority Report (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Predestination?
faq.answer: Try Minority Report, Source Code, Children of Men—each shares DNA with Predestination while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Predestination?
faq.answer: Predestination (2014) is best described as Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Predestination worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
